sections.paragraph:
σϰοτία, ας, ἡ [σκότος]

—1. ‘condition prevailing when it is night’, darkness J 6:17; 20:1; metaph., of someth. said outside another’s hearing Mt 10:27; Lk 12:3. On J 12:35b s. below.

—2. ‘inward state or condition amounting to ignorance or benightedness in moral/spiritual matters’, darkness Mt 4:16 v.l.; J 1:5; 8:12; 12:35a (vs. 35b appears to be a practical illustration), 46; 1 J 1:5; 2:8f, 11.
